steps: Let b = morning loaves. After selling 2/3 before lunch, loaves left = b - (2b/3) = b/3. Then she bakes 10 more: b/3 + 10. Then sells 15: b/3 + 10 - 15. Remaining = 13. So equation: b/3 + 10 - 15 = 13. Simplify: b/3 - 5 = 13 → b/3 = 18 → b = 54. Check options: A matches equation b - (2b/3) + 10 - 15 = 13, which simplifies to b/3 - 5 = 13, and gives b = 54. B same equation but b = 36, wrong. C: (2b/3) + 10 - 15 = 13 → 2b/3 - 5 = 13 → 2b/3 = 18 → b = 27, but equation is wrong because it omits morning loaves left after selling.
